authorization.scope: narrow provisional-autosave correction discovered before autosave-only publication

discovered_gaps.base: The frozen wire permits a provisional autosave with no manual base, while the entry, receipt rule, and durability brief incorrectly imply that every autosave has a manual base.
discovered_gaps.publication: The contract defines and independently fixtures the provisional state but does not freeze how its first autosave-only package is installed or retried.
discovered_gaps.risk: An implementation could reject the accepted provisional wire, invent a second identity or recovery path, overwrite an existing destination, or misreport a base that does not exist.

correction.base_rule: An established-project autosave requires the current manual generation as its base. A provisional autosave has no manual base in its generation, head ref, capture, or receipt.
correction.destination: The first private provisional publication takes one caller-supplied validated ProjectStorePath. The store neither chooses the application recovery directory nor invents another ProjectId.
correction.first_capture: The first provisional capture requires expected_parent=null, autosave_base=null, forked_from=null, and a projection whose ProjectId equals the envelope ProjectId.
correction.first_publication: Create generation sequence zero with kind=autosave and base_manual_generation_id=null; publish its complete immutable closure and generation; publish only refs/autosave-head with previous and base absent; fully validate and sync a sibling package; install it with no-clobber rename; then sync the destination parent.
correction.advance: Advancing a provisional store requires expected_parent equal to the current autosave head, autosave_base=null, matching project/scientific identity and fork facts, and a non-regressing revision high-water. It uses the accepted autosave recovery-before-head protocol and remains provisional.
correction.receipt: A provisional autosave receipt carries autosave_base_generation_id=null. An established autosave receipt carries its required manual base. Neither can advance saved_revision.
correction.retry: Never overwrite an existing destination. A fresh retry may adopt only an exact fully validated destination created by the same first capture and must complete its required destination-parent sync; an unrelated or mismatched destination fails closed.
correction.cancellation: First-publication cancellation before package install removes only the owned sibling stage and changes no destination. During advance, cancellation before recovery-ref publication may leave validated immutable orphans, and cancellation after durable recovery-ref publication may leave only the accepted recovery-ahead state while the old autosave head remains authoritative. Cancellation is deferred across package install and each ref replacement through required syncs. Post-authority uncertainty returns CommitIndeterminate, suspends writes, and requires fresh reopen/retry.
correction.transitions: No new transition name is added. First publication composes the existing commit_prefix, object, and generation sets, the autosave-parameterized head_ref set, package_stage_create, package_tree_sync, package_install_noreplace, and destination_parent_sync. Advance composes the existing commit_prefix, object, and generation sets plus the autosave-parameterized recovery_ref and head_ref sets.
correction.timer_owner: B2 publishes and coalesces explicitly submitted captures only. The B3 application integration owns the 30-second idle and 120-second maximum scheduling because the application owns live revision and capture creation. Exact trigger, reset, and monotonic-clock semantics remain unfrozen; this correction authorizes no timer implementation.

superseded_fields: PROJECT_STORE_DURABILITY_BRIEF.md statement that every autosave records a manual base
unchanged: the sixteen public root exports and every command, completion, fault, and limit
every persisted path and byte plus the accepted provisional fixture
dependencies, product reachability, and B4 deletion gate
public actor construction, initial-manual handoff, dead-writer staging cleanup, and filesystem durability remain later work
evidence: exact correction binding and normalized-contract checks
unchanged independent provisional fixture, public root, dependencies, wire bytes, limits, and product reachability
future focused first-publication, advance, cancellation, collision, indeterminacy, and fresh-retry tests
stop_condition: Any public command/completion/fault/export change, persisted byte or path change, new transition, dependency, fixture mutation, product wiring, provisional cleanup, timer implementation, or durability claim must stop for separate review.
rollback: revert this correction before provisional publication implementation; the project store remains off-product
